description:
You are a machine-learning engineer doing a quick, single-command benchmark before you start model training.  
    The goal is to:

    1. Sort an existing raw CSV file by its first (integer) column.  
    2. Write the sorted data to a *new* file.  
    3. Capture a very small “benchmark” that compares the byte-sizes of the original and the sorted files.

    Environment
    • Your home directory is /home/user  
    • The raw data file already exists at  
      /home/user/dataset/raw.csv  
    • The file’s exact current contents are:  
      id,value  
      3,apple  
      1,orange  
      2,banana  

    What has to be produced
    1. A new, **sorted** file located at  
       /home/user/dataset/sorted_raw.csv  
       – It must contain the same header line followed by all data lines sorted in *ascending* order of the first column.  
       – The expected final contents (including the trailing newline on every line) must be **exactly**:  
         id,value  
         1,orange  
         2,banana  
         3,apple  

    2. A benchmark log located at  
       /home/user/benchmark/sort_benchmark.log  
       – Create the directory /home/user/benchmark if it does not already exist.  
       – The log must contain exactly **two** newline-terminated lines, in this order and with no extra whitespace:  
         raw_size_bytes=35  
         sorted_size_bytes=35  

         (Those byte counts are correct for the given example files, so your command must output them exactly.)

    Constraints
    • Achieve everything with **one single compound shell command** typed at the terminal prompt.  
    • Do not hard-code the numbers 35 into the log; instead, compute them in your command so that the log would stay correct even if the input size changes.  
    • You are not asked to time the execution—only to compare file sizes.  
    • Do not modify /home/user/dataset/raw.csv in any way.  
    • Aside from creating /home/user/dataset/sorted_raw.csv and /home/user/benchmark/sort_benchmark.log, no other files should be created or altered.

    Verification notes
    • Automated tests will fail if either file is missing, has the wrong path, wrong name, wrong line order, or wrong byte-counts in the log.  
    • The test harness will read /home/user/dataset/sorted_raw.csv and compare it byte-for-byte against the expected contents shown above.  
    • The test harness will read /home/user/benchmark/sort_benchmark.log and expect it to contain exactly the two lines shown above—no leading/trailing spaces, exactly one newline character at the end of each line.
truth:
Initial file tree:

    /home/user/dataset/raw.csv
        Contents (including trailing newlines):
            id,value\n
            3,apple\n
            1,orange\n
            2,banana\n

    After correct task completion the following additional artefacts must exist:

    1. /home/user/dataset/sorted_raw.csv  
       Contents (including trailing newlines, no extra blank lines):
            id,value\n
            1,orange\n
            2,banana\n
            3,apple\n

    2. /home/user/benchmark/sort_benchmark.log  
       Exact contents (two lines, each terminated by a single newline, no extra whitespace):
            raw_size_bytes=35\n
            sorted_size_bytes=35\n
